scope: Current tracked Markdown files under docs/message_board plus adjacent content-pipeline references.
strategy: Classify active workspace documents by audience, freshness, and disposition before broader Markdown corpus reorganization.

classification_rules.active_handoff: Current session state, handoff, or active triage that future AI collaborators should read first.
classification_rules.active_working: Current-cycle plan or checklist that may remain in docs/message_board until the cycle closes.
classification_rules.internal_strategy: Strategic or operational material useful to the project but not suitable as public-facing documentation without review.
classification_rules.grant_internal: Grant, funding, LLC, banking, or application-strategy material. Directionally useful but stale and internal.
classification_rules.publication_candidate: Material that may become a paper, whitepaper, public article, or site page after claim hygiene.
classification_rules.historical_archive: Completed release, audit, implementation, or triage artifact that should move to docs/archive or a topical historical folder when cleanup proceeds.
classification_rules.external_integration: Notes about adjacent projects or integrations that should stay separate from WhiteMagic unless explicitly revived.

epistemic_framework.Established: Verified code, tests, release artifacts, or factual project state.
epistemic_framework.Emerging: Useful strategic or research direction with partial support.
epistemic_framework.Speculative: Forward-looking or unvalidated; requires claim hygiene before public use.
epistemic_framework.Mythopoetic: Symbolic, Garden, or private ontology material; not empirical public documentation.

active_handoff.rationale: Master handoff now has a May 21 current-status addendum and should remain the first active message-board entry for agents.
Current deferred cleanup map from Fragment + STRATA audit findings; still relevant for code-hygiene follow-up.
Recent verified state report that remains useful as bridge context, but should be superseded by the May 21 handoff for live baselines.

active_working.rationale: Master roadmap for the May objective cycle; status should be refreshed before public reuse.
May execution sprint plan; time-sensitive and useful internally, but stale day-by-day assumptions require review.
Operational checklist for whitemagic.dev launch work.
Session state tracking for site/librarian work; useful as active operational context if that stream resumes.
Core shipping surface audit; active implementation reference but should be reconciled with current truth-spine updates.

internal_strategy.rationale: Strategic analysis is useful but includes product/funding positioning that should remain internal until rewritten.
Growth strategy tiers are internal planning material and should not be treated as public claims without review.
CODEX synthesis is valuable strategic source material but should be claim-checked before public or grant use.
Coordination notice for a prior git-history migration; likely historical/internal unless that migration is active again.

grant_internal.rationale: Operational grant plan is directionally useful but stale relative to the user's May 20 strategy comments.
Grant pipeline tracker contains time-sensitive status and should be refreshed or moved internal.
Exploratory grant analysis includes probabilities and funder tactics that are internal and stale.
Grant prioritization is internal planning and should be refreshed against newer WhiteMagic Labs strategy.
Reusable grant templates are internal drafting material; metrics and framing need Option C review before reuse.
Canonical grant boilerplate is useful but must be updated for Option C metrics and new strategy before use.
Rubric audit is valuable internal review material; current checklist still references older metric assumptions.
Grant-writing guide is internal training material and should be refreshed before reuse.
Federal grant playbook is long-horizon internal strategy with administrative details; not public-facing.

publication_candidate.rationale: High-value whitepaper/preprint seed for declared-vs-actual side-effect audit infrastructure.
Useful evidence map and strategic prior-art analysis; needs claim hygiene before public citation.
Competitive landscape reference that can support public positioning after verification refresh.
Release impact narrative may become public release/history material after Option C language review.
Release readiness report is historically useful and could support public release notes.

historical_archive.rationale: Large historical audit from earlier stabilization work; superseded for live state by current handoff.
Resolved stub audit; important history but no longer active workspace material.
Stub scout report for resolved cleanup; preserve as historical reference.
Completed plan; preserve as historical reference and archive later.
Completed v22.2 roadmap; historical release artifact.
Older code-quality review superseded by later audits and current tests.
Old failure triage from broken baseline; superseded by green current suite.
Completed audit report from early April; superseded by later release/handoff docs.
Completed implementation report from early April; superseded by later handoffs.
Administrative roadmap is time-sensitive and no longer active public workspace material.

external_integration.rationale: Adjacent-project integration note. Edge Chat should remain separate unless explicitly revived.

recommended_next_actions: Keep only active_handoff and active_working documents in docs/message_board after the next move pass.
Move grant_internal materials to a non-public internal grants folder or local-only private strategy folder after confirming desired visibility.
Move historical_archive materials to docs/archive with topical subfolders and update INDEX.md in the same change.
Promote publication_candidate materials only after claim hygiene, Option C metric review, and public/private audience review.
Do not merge Fragment, STRATA, Edge Chat, or other auxiliary projects into WhiteMagic; use them externally or as independent tools.
